comments: First 30 minutes of show missing, tape cuts in during second track of FNC.
From a collection of 274 tapes mixing John Peel shows, Friday Rock Shows and Alan Freeman Saturday Shows from 1978 to 1983. The Derby Box was acquired by the John Peel Yahoo Group.

sessions.details: ‘Dark Side Of The Moon‘ recorded live at Wembley 16th November 1974.

track_listing.details: LP-Life On The Ceiling; Criminal Records
LP-MMPH - More Miles Per Hour; Decca

track_listing.track: End of show and Midnight News

transcript.text: And that's our Friday night connection for this week. And may I say a really nice one. Andy Cottrell of Mapleton, Carriage Drive, Frodsham & Cheshire. Seasonal as well. And just so we don't give the impression that we're trying to lead people astray, the last piece of music in that triptych was just an excerpt of the track that normally lasts about 12 minutes long.
now if you think you can work out what the connection is what the theme is between those
three pieces of music put down the answer on a postcard back of an envelope 10 pound note anything
you like and put together your own friday night connection as well because that's really important
okay and send it along to tommy vance friday night connection bbc radio one london w1a4 ww
and the first card out of the bag with the correct answer and the tastiest connection
will get a £10 record token.

join me then.
David Essex presents Star Special
this Sunday on Radio 1 at 7pm.
That's one of the many fine musicians
who's come out of the northeast from the album more miles per hour that was john miles and his
band and we all fall down and before that attract from a new album that's just come out it's on

behind it michael chapman who to me is one of the real greats in this country he's an absolutely
fascinating character i wonder if you remember the last album that he brought out it was a guitar
tutor album that also had a book that went along with it if you are into sort of learning the
guitar or making your expertise even better get hold of it because it's got some fabulous pieces
of music on it michael chapman superstar now here's a fast talking fella saturday afternoon at four on

the one you can buy anywhere but in Britain.
Bram Tchaikovsky has some harsh things to say
about the music press. And there's good
news about Bob Dylan. All in
Rock On. Radio 1, 275,

Saturday at 4.
Never get anywhere with a voice like
that, son. Don't miss the program tomorrow.
It's going to be very, very good. The time now
is 7 minutes before 11 o'clock.
The Floyd, just after 11 o'clock,
doing Dark Side of the Moon, as recorded in Wembley in 1974.
That's Boston with their new single,
which of course has been lifted from their last album,
which was called Don't Look Back.
The single is feeling satisfied.
And before that, you heard Graham Parker and The Rumour
with a track from their new album, Squeezing Out Sparks.
It's out on Vertigo, and the cut was called Local Girls.
It's exactly 11 o'clock.
To put you in the mood for Dark Side of the Moon,
starting very, very soon,
Here's Steve Hillage and an extract from Garden of Paradise
from his album Rainbow Dome Music.
And that's Steve Hillage from the album Rainbow Dome Music,
which incidentally is not the follow-up to Live Herald.
It's the music specially put together for the festival for the mind, the body, and the spirit,
which is on at Olympia between the 21st and the 29th of April.
And it's very well-priced, and it's on Virgin.
And now, let's go back to 1974, to Wembley, and relive 53 minutes of The Dark Side of the Moon, Pink Floyd.
The Pink Floyd, as recorded on Saturday the 16th of November, 1974, at Wembley, doing Dark Side of the Moon.
Produced by Tony Wilson on that day, and we do trust that you enjoyed it,
as indeed is the Friday Rock Show produced by Tony Wilson.
He's our very own Easter Bunny.
Next week on the Friday Rock Show, a classic session by family.
Don't forget Rock On tomorrow, 4 o'clock on Radio 1,
and Peebles and Peely weeknights on 1.
Our engineers tonight were Dave and Simon.
Thank you, gents.
Trust you're having a really pleasant Easter holiday.
I'll see you tomorrow at 4 for Rock On.
Till then, take care of yourselves and God bless.
